steps.assistant_text: rmon register-dump command handler (kernel_022_f, IDO 7.1 -O1). Matched byte-exact from a ~68.6% structural decode via four levers: (1) register-pin the arg pointer to s0 and loop index to s1 ('register char *s0 = arg0;' / 'register int i;') so they live across the 32-iteration MMIO-read loop and the 8 fixed-register reads instead of being spilled/reloaded each callee call; (2) model the reply buffer as ONE contiguous RmonRegDump struct (header fields at +4/+6/+0xC, regs[40] at +0x10) passed whole as &dump, matching the single sp+0x24 base IDO holds for header stores and the loop's sp+0x34 array base; (3) call func_800073F8 DIRECTLY (typed extern, not a fn-ptr cast) so it emits 'jal' with R_MIPS_26 rather than lui/addiu/jalr; (4) declaration order 'register s0; struct dump; register i;' pins the struct to sp+0x24 — struct-FIRST overshoots to +0x28 (8-aligned), struct-LAST undershoots to +0x20, and interposing exactly the s0 register decl before the struct lands the 4-aligned +0x24 slot. Also flipped the entry guard to 'if (func_80008430() != 0) return -4;' (beqz form) and called func_80008430 with no explicit arg (K&R). .text 0x160 byte-identical; full make clean && make ROM cmp clean vs baserom.

metadata.verification: Full make clean && make produces tenshoe.z64 byte-identical to baserom.z64 (cmp clean). func_80009314 .text (0x160, 88 words) byte-identical AND reloc-identical to expected/src/kernel/kernel_022_f.c.o. Real build path is now compiled C (INCLUDE_ASM removed).
